_comment: Phase branch 2026-July-RayDPT-fast. Forked from 2026-July-RayDPT-validation at commit 040f0ed with all 15 experiments, study conclusions, archive, ideas and discrepancies intact -- the validation branch is read-only history and its numbers remain comparable (same evaluator, split, target, score formula, and the same 3600s scored budget). What changes here is only train.py's DEFAULTS and two measured architecture knobs. Nothing about the benchmark moves.
reset_note: Fresh from original baseline f677b0f + mask-weighted coarse/low loss targets + composite abs_rel weight 0.75->0.35 + PLANAR (cubemap) depth target.
depth_convention: planar (cubemap perpendicular-Z); stored erp_depth used as-is; radial_factor removed
metric: composite (selection) = rmse/1.6 + (1-d1)/0.46 + 0.35*abs_rel  (lower better; RMSE+d1 dominate; abs_rel de-weighted)

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: Before any RayDPT work the BatVision U-Net reference must be re-anchored under the PLANAR depth target, and we must know which binaural input representation the reference actually needs. Two questions: (a) how much do the derived interaural cues (ILD, cosIPD, sinIPD) add over the bare [L,R] magnitude pair; (b) does log1p magnitude compression help or hurt.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: log1p compression equalises the dynamic range between the direct sound and the far-field echo tail, so it should help a model that must read weak late reflections to place distant surfaces -> log wins, and it should help the 2ch stack MORE than the 5ch stack (in 5ch, ILD already supplies a log-domain ratio, so part of the compression benefit is redundant). Adding ILD+IPD gives explicit interaural level/phase, which is where azimuth and hence per-direction depth structure lives -> 5ch beats 2ch at both log settings.
concluded_studies.experiment_note: 2x2 grid on run_base.py, 1h each, sequential on one GPU: {2ch=[L,R], 5ch=[L,R,ILD,cosIPD,sinIPD]} x {use_log True, False}. E0 batvision_2ch_nolog, E1 batvision_2ch_log, E2 batvision_5ch_nolog, E3 batvision_5ch_log. Driver: utils/run_batvision_grid.sh.
observations: E1 vs E0 (2ch, log vs nolog): composite 1.8784 vs 1.8854, delta = 0.0070 -- BELOW the sigma ~= 0.008 noise floor. log1p compression on the [L,R] magnitude pair is INDISTINGUISHABLE from raw magnitude at 2ch. The two runs also split the underlying metrics (log wins rmse 1.3116 vs 1.3186 and d1 0.5808 vs 0.5785; nolog wins abs_rel 0.4143 vs 0.4211), which is the signature of noise, not of a mechanism. Do NOT crown either. The detailed hypothesis predicted log would help MORE at 2ch than 5ch (no ILD to supply a log-domain ratio); at 2ch it does not help at all, so that half of the prediction is already not supported. The 5ch pair (E2/E3) still tests the other half.

Both 2ch cells peak at epoch 14 of 26 and both peak VRAM at exactly 2400.3MB -- the overfitting turn and the memory envelope are properties of the architecture + schedule, not of the input representation.

E0: the composite peaks at epoch 14 of 26 and then DEGRADES -- val loss rises monotonically 0.1871 -> 0.1925 over the last 5 epochs, RMSE 1.347 -> 1.382, d1 0.5827 -> 0.5758. The batvision U-Net (54.4M params) OVERFITS well inside the 1-hour budget. Compounding it: the cosine schedule is sized for --epochs 40 but only ~26 fit in the budget, so LR never fully anneals and the run ends mid-schedule at a degraded point. Best-checkpoint selection protects the reported number, but it means (a) the extra epochs are wasted compute, and (b) a fair comparison of any config must go through the best checkpoint, never the last. FOLLOW-UP for the training-optimization lineage: set --epochs to what actually fits (~24) so cosine anneals to zero inside the budget, and/or add weight decay / early stop. Do NOT change this mid-grid -- all four cells must share one schedule.
concluded_studies.decision_rule: Rank by the honest composite. The winning cell becomes the reference number RayDPT must beat. The qualitative figure shows ONE column per channel count, always the NON-LOG variant (operator instruction), regardless of which log setting wins the composite; the log variants stay in out/results.tsv. Gaps below ~0.015 composite are near-noise -> do not crown without confirmation.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: RayDPT (my ray-conditioned model) must be re-anchored under the PLANAR target before any of its mechanisms can be judged. Nothing measured before commit 87b3047 is comparable, so the model currently has NO valid baseline in this phase and no RayDPT improvement can be attributed.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: Run train.py unchanged (5ch log, flip-aug, no TTA) for one wall-clock hour. This produces the parent against which decoder, representation and optimisation mechanisms will be compared, and gives the first honest RayDPT-vs-batvision number under the planar target. The reference to beat is E3 = 1.8567.
concluded_studies.experiment_note: E4: python train.py --mode train (defaults). Queued in utils/run_queue.sh; started 04:28.
concluded_studies.decision_rule: This is an ANCHOR, not a competition: E4 is recorded whatever it scores. Report which metrics RayDPT wins/loses vs E3 separately (per S0, d1 is the cue-driven metric and rmse the range metric). If RayDPT trails badly, that is a research finding about ray-conditioning under a piecewise-constant target, not a failure to be patched immediately.
observations: RayDPT is COMPUTE-STARVED: 5 epochs vs batvision's 25 under the same wall-clock budget, best checkpoint = last epoch, all val metrics still improving. The 2.0471 vs 1.8567 gap measures budget, not architecture. 64x128 attention (lsa64 + cross64) is 57% of forward cost; F.unfold in _window_kv materialises 1.8GB at batch 16.
68.7% of RayDPT's parameters (e5..e8, 16.78M of 24.44M) are dead: never used in forward, no gradients.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: Under a wall-clock budget, compute spent per step must be repaid in epochs. A model that cannot converge inside the budget is not being evaluated on its mechanism -- it is being penalised for its speed. Before RayDPT's ray-conditioning can be judged at all, RayDPT must be able to converge.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: E4 fitted 5 epochs at 713 s/epoch, best checkpoint = last epoch, all val metrics still improving. Profiling shows cross-attention is 67% of forward, and I2's oracle shows the 64x128 decode scale imposes a ceiling (composite 0.3527 even at 32x64) that the model, at 2.0471, is nowhere near. Cutting the decode scale to 32x64 and the cross-attention to one layer per scale gives a MEASURED 147.3 s/epoch = 24.4 epochs/h, while preserving ray-conditioning. A converged cheap ray model should beat an unconverged expensive one.
concluded_studies.experiment_note: E9 raydpt_e9_d32L1_b64: --decode-scale 32 --ray-cross-layers 1 --batch-size 64 --lr 1.2e-3 (linear scaling) --amp bf16 --epochs 25. COMPOUND change; attribute nothing to a single part.
concluded_studies.decision_rule: The question is convergence, not victory. Success = epochs_ran approaches 25 AND the best epoch is no longer the last, i.e. the run actually converges. If the composite also beats E4's 2.0471, D5 is confirmed and RayDPT was merely starved. If it converges but scores WORSE than E4, then the capacity we removed was load-bearing and the cut is the wrong one -- record that, do not patch it away. Ablate the compound change only after it is known to converge.
observations: Converged: best epoch 18/21, val loss flat over the last 4 epochs. +0.1162 over the starved E4 = 14 sigma. D5 confirmed.
21 epochs, not the 24.4 the throughput bench predicted: the bench timed train steps only, and validation costs ~14 s/epoch. Benchmarks that omit validation overstate epochs-fit.
Still 0.0741 behind batvision, and 0.0691 of the gap is d1. RMSE is tied. See D9.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: E9 is a COMPOUND change, so its deficit against the reference cannot be attributed. Before asking whether ray-conditioning helps, we must know which part of the capacity we removed was load-bearing -- otherwise we would be judging a mechanism by an amputation we chose ourselves.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: E9 cut both the decode scale (64->32) and the cross-attention depth (2->1 layers per ray scale). The audio<->ray cross-attention is where per-ray direction information enters the model, so halving it is the change most likely to have cost the d1 (angular) deficit seen in D9. Restoring ray_cross_layers=2 at decode 32 still fits the budget (measured 216.5 s/epoch = 16.6 epochs/h).
concluded_studies.decision_rule: Judge on d1 first, not the composite: D9 says the gap is angular. If d1 recovers materially toward 0.5949, the cross-layer cut was load-bearing. If d1 does not move, the cut was free and the deficit lies in the decode scale, in raw capacity, or in ray-conditioning itself. Report epochs_ran; fewer epochs fit at 2 layers, which is itself a confound (D2/D5).
concluded_studies.conclusion: INCONCLUSIVE (E10 did not converge; the question stands)
observations: d1 moved only +0.0034 (12% of the gap to batvision, below sigma), but E10's best checkpoint is the LAST epoch -- it never converged, so its d1 is a lower bound and S4's question is unanswered.
E10's 15 UNCONVERGED epochs at 2 layers beat E9's 21 CONVERGED epochs at 1 layer (1.9192 vs 1.9308, above sigma). Capacity helps per epoch; the wall-clock budget takes it back.

The real finding is the trade-off: decode64 L2 -> 5 ep (no converge); decode32 L2 -> 15 ep (no converge); decode32 L1 -> 21 ep (converged, weaker). All lose to a 54.4M U-Net that converges in 25. RayDPT's binding constraint is the COST of a ray cross-attention layer, not the number of them.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: D9 claims RayDPT's entire deficit against the reference is ANGULAR (d1), which would indict ray-conditioning at exactly the point it claims to win. But that claim rests on models that never finished learning: no 2-layer RayDPT has ever converged inside the budget. Before indicting the mechanism, read d1 on a converged 2-layer model.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: Benchmarking showed the cost of a cross layer is not its FFN (ffn 4->2 saves 2 s/epoch) but its KV set: cr32 attends 2048 e3 tokens. Shrinking that KV to e4's 512 coarse tokens is 4x cheaper at IDENTICAL parameter count (5.89M), giving a measured 145.8 s/epoch = 24.7 epochs. If d1 then recovers toward batvision's 0.5949, D9 was a budget artefact (H2). If d1 stays near 0.566 on a CONVERGED 2-layer model, the angular deficit belongs to ray-conditioning itself (H1).
concluded_studies.decision_rule: Convergence first: best_epoch must not be the last, or the run answers nothing (as E10 did). Then read d1, NOT the composite -- D9 is an angular claim. Caveat to honour: kv=e4 is a mechanism change, so if d1 recovers, credit is shared between '2 layers' and 'coarse KV' and must be ablated before any claim.
confounds: kv=e4 changes which audio tokens the 32-scale rays attend to (2048 fine -> 512 coarse). Not a free speedup.
H7 was refuted on the full val set: an oracle per-image scale closes only 12% of the d1 gap, so the deficit is genuinely angular.
D10: all diagnostics must now use the full val set; small samples reversed the sign of both d1 and the spectrum.
concluded_studies.conclusion: PASS with reframing (2x2 attributed; D9 shown not to be angular)
observations: 2x2 attribution: coarse KV gives d1 +0.0033, the second cross layer +0.0046; additive, no interaction, and neither alone clears sigma.
The spatial decomposition overturned D9: the deficit is far-field range compression, not angles. H1/H4 mis-specified; H6/H7 refuted at zero GPU.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: A loss chooses what a model hedges toward. Masked MAE drives every pixel to the conditional median; where far depths are a minority of the mass, the median sits short and the model systematically under-predicts distance. d1 is a relative threshold, so that shortfall is fatal to it.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: Charge relative error instead: |D-gt|/gt, or the symmetric |log D - log gt|. Both price a 25% mistake identically at 1 m and 9 m, which is exactly what d1 measures. Expect d1 to rise, driven by the 7-10 m deciles, and RMSE to worsen slightly.
concluded_studies.experiment_note: E13 raydpt_e13_relmae, E14 raydpt_e14_logmae -- E11's architecture exactly, only the dense term changes.
concluded_studies.decision_rule: Judge on d1 and RMSE. ABS_REL improves by construction and is NOT evidence (program.md). Falsification: the 7-10 m deciles do not improve -> drop I13 whatever the composite does. Re-run utils/diag_d1.py on the winner before crowning.
observations: Both relative dense terms made the 7-10m deciles WORSE. The dense MAE is not the cause of far-field compression.

rel_mae's 1/gt weighting moved the error budget to NEAR pixels: its 0-1m d1 (0.4987) beats every model including batvision (0.2890), while its 9-10m prediction collapses to 3.814m. The sign error, reproduced in the data.
log_mae is symmetric in the ratio and still hurts, so relativity itself -- not rel_mae's asymmetry -- is wrong here.
The diagnosis survives the failed remedy: far-field compression is measured, and it is where d1 is lost.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: Research throughput is a first-class research variable. Under a fixed wall-clock budget, a cheaper model that converges further is not merely faster to study -- it scores better, and it lets more hypotheses be tested per day. But a speed change that quietly costs accuracy buys nothing, so every knob must be paid for in a scored run.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: GPU profiling of the E11 champion (batch 64, bf16): cr32 37.1%, lsa32 33.5%, enc 13.9%, cr16 10.0% of forward. Two knobs follow. raydpt_win32 5->3 shrinks the local spherical attention from 25 offsets to 9 (measured 1.20x). ffn_mult 4->2 halves the CrossBlock FFN (1.10x on top). Together 169.1 -> 127.7 s/epoch, 21.3 -> 28.2 epochs/h, 5.89M -> 5.29M params.
concluded_studies.experiment_note: E17 raydpt_e17_fastbase: the new defaults, scored at the full 3600s budget, --epochs 28.
concluded_studies.decision_rule: Adopt iff E17's composite is within sigma (0.008) of E11's 1.9093, or better. Both knobs are CAPACITY cuts, so speed alone proves nothing. A win does not prove the knobs are free -- it proves the trade is favourable at this budget. If E17 loses by more than sigma, revert win32/ffn to E11's values and keep only the reorganised defaults.
concluded_studies.conclusion: FAIL (E17 diverged; the fast knobs are unvalidated)
observations: The 1.32x speedup (win32 5->3, ffn 4->2) is measured; its accuracy price is UNPAID because both runs that used it diverged.
E17 and E15 diverged identically. E18 removed the auxiliary entirely and diverged anyway -- so lc is a symptom, not the cause. The instability is in the trunk.
The real obstacle to fast experimentation is that lr 1.2e-3 sits on the edge: every architecture change tips it over. Efficiency is blocked on optimisation, not FLOPs.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: Depth from echoes is a time-of-flight measurement: a surface at depth d returns its echo at t = 2d/c. A network that regresses a scalar depth must discover that correspondence from data; a network whose architecture encodes it does not. Put the physics in the structure, not in the loss.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: The encoder's width axis IS time -- spec is (freq 256, time 512), so e3 is (freq 32, time 64) and its columns are depth hypotheses spanning 0.08-9.92 m. Each ray attends over FREQUENCY within one time column per hypothesis, a small MLP scores the hypotheses, and a softmax over the DEPTH axis gives p(d|ray); depth = soft-argmax over echo delay. It also replaces the sigmoid coarse head that saturated in D11.
concluded_studies.experiment_note: E24 = defaults + --depth-volume True (control E21). E25 = champion architecture + depth volume (control E23). E26 = confirm draw.
concluded_studies.decision_rule: The pre-registered falsification (far deciles must improve) PASSED. Crowning still requires (a) the mechanism measured on the champion architecture, since E24 carried knobs that cost 0.0137, and (b) a confirming draw -- program.md forbids crowning on one. Judge on the far deciles and d1; ABS_REL is not evidence.
concluded_studies.conclusion: PASS as a scoped hypothesis, FAIL as a champion
observations: EchoDelayVolume's pre-registered far-decile prediction held on the FAST parent (E24) and FAILED on the CHAMPION parent (E27), on a converged, fully-annealed run.

Cause: the champion's wider LSA (win5) and larger FFN (ffn4) already provide far-field accuracy (E23 8-9m d1 0.2306 vs fast 0.1845), so the mechanism competes with existing capacity rather than adding to it.
A specialist that helps a starved decoder and hinders a strong one. Real, reproducible, scoped -- but not a champion.

concluded_studies.general_hypothesis: The echo-delay cost volume is the project's one confirmed mechanism (pre-registered far-decile prediction held on the fast parent). Its through-line is time resolution: every far-field result -- E9/E12 fine tokens, E24 itself, batvision's full-res skips -- says the lever is resolving echo arrival time finely. So push the volume's own time resolution, and pair it with a second far-field path.
concluded_studies.detailed_hypothesis: E29 (I20): read e2 (time 128) for 2x delay resolution. E30 (I21): combine with fine-token routing (cross_kv32=e3). Both extend E24 on the fast parent, control E24 (1.8987), each differing in one variable.
concluded_studies.experiment_note: E29 --depth-volume-src e2; E30 --cross-kv32 e3. Both on win32=3 ffn=2 lr 3e-4.
concluded_studies.decision_rule: Pre-registered, per idea I19's line: the 7-10m deciles must improve over E24, or drop. Judge on far deciles and d1; ABS_REL is not evidence. E30 additionally must not diverge (D11).
concluded_studies.conclusion: FAIL (both pre-registered far-decile tests failed); EchoDelayVolume saturates
observations: Finer volume time bins (e2) did not improve the far field: the volume's 64 columns already out-resolve the achieved RMSE.
EchoDelayVolume + kv=e3 do not compose: same signal, different path -- redundant like E27's EDE+dense.
D13: far-field time-resolution structure in the DECODER has saturated. The limit is upstream (encoder pooling) or the sensor (I7).
Side gain: kv=e3 is stable at lr 3e-4, reopening a knob D11 had closed.

mode_reason: D13 named the exact next test -- read the STFT directly, bypassing encoder pooling -- so no divergence checkpoint is needed; the decisive experiment is already specified.

active_study.general_hypothesis: D13 showed decoder-side far-field time structure saturates at e3's 64 columns. The remaining question is binary: is the limit the ENCODER (which pooled 512->64 before the volume read it) or the SENSOR (two-mic observability, I7)?
active_study.detailed_hypothesis: Read the STFT spec directly (512 time columns, 2 cm depth spacing, encoder bypassed). Improvement over E24 => encoder-limited. Saturation => sensor-limited, closing the time-resolution line.
active_study.experiment_note: E31 raydpt_e31_ede_raw: --depth-volume-src raw on the fast parent.
active_study.decision_rule: Pre-registered: the 7-10m deciles must improve over E24 (1.8987), or the entire time-resolution line closes and the far field is declared sensor-limited (a scoped ceiling, I7). Judge on far deciles and d1.

backlog.idea: E4: re-anchor RayDPT (train.py, 5ch log, flip-aug) under the planar target; this is the number the batvision grid is compared against. Must run before any RayDPT improvement.
RayDPT decodes at 64x128 and bilinear-upsamples 4x to 256x512; batvision's U-Net decodes to full res. Add a 128x256 DPT scale (ray bank Q128 + se1 skip from e1) so the final upsample is only 2x.
backlog.why: MEASURED (24 samples, 6 scenes): the planar target is PIECEWISE-CONSTANT -- a wall perpendicular to a cube face has constant planar depth, so the map is flat regions separated by SHARP steps at depth discontinuities. A 4x bilinear upsample smears exactly those steps, and d1 (a +-25% threshold metric) punishes blurred edges hard. Highest-value structural fix under the planar target. Costs VRAM -- screen before committing.

backlog.idea: Predict the smooth radial field R and emit D = R * linf, linf = max(|dx|,|dy|,|dz|) as a fixed buffer, so the net never learns the cube-edge creases.
backlog.why_refuted: The premise is false. Measured grad-energy over 24 samples / 6 scenes: planar 0.01618, radial 0.01677; radial is smoother in only 17% of samples (mean ratio 1.035). PLANAR is already the smoother parameterisation -- for an axis-aligned room a wall perpendicular to a face has CONSTANT planar depth while radial curves as 1/cos. Reparameterising to radial would make the regression HARDER, not easier. Do not revive without new evidence.

backlog.idea: Match --epochs to what the 1-hour budget actually fits (~24-26) so the cosine schedule anneals to zero inside the budget instead of being truncated mid-schedule. Observed on E0: best at epoch 14/26, then 12 epochs of overfitting (val loss 0.1871 -> 0.1925). Pair with weight decay / EMA.
Re-validate the June optimisation envelope under the fixed loss + planar target: bf16 AMP + bs32 + lr4e-4 + warmup-cosine + EMA0.995.
Re-validate the June representation wins: multi-res STFT (fine-time echo delay) + interaural coherence (direct-vs-diffuse).
Drop the vestigial pix2pix encoder tail (24.44M->7.69M, provably equivalent) - re-apply early once baseline confirmed.

fork_note: RayDPT re-initialised from the E11 champion (composite 1.9093, d1 0.5710, 23 epochs, CONVERGED) rather than from the original E4 anchor, which never converged. Defaults now reproduce E11 plus two profiler-selected knobs (raydpt_win32 3, ffn_mult 2).
